what_it_does: Acceptance test file for Suite.blast_radius that exercises the X9 nearly-free co-reference blast radius: it verifies blast_radius inverts the existing code-symbols.json referenced_by index into sibling addresses/features, reusing resolve_scope's forward symbol lookup, while enforcing self-exclusion, honest empty results, fail-loud malformed-address gating, stale-corpus handling, feature referrer inclusion, a shared_only knob, and crash-free behaviour across every real corpus address.
observations: Module-level script with a global PASS counter and a custom check(label, cond) helper that asserts and prints ok lines.
Builds a temporary controlled code-symbols.json fixture and shadows Suite._corpus_dir to redirect both resolve_scope and blast_radius to it.
Defines fixture addresses A, B, C and symbols code://shared/S and code://lonely/T, plus a non-ui ENG-feat referrer.
Numbered checks cover: 1 co-reference + self-exclusion, 5 feature inclusion, 2 honest empty, 7 shared_only knob, 3 S0 gate + resolve_scope preservation, 4 stale handling, 6 real-corpus robustness.
Verifies resolve_scope still returns the same symbols and scope and still raises on malformed addresses after blast_radius exists.
Uses a real corpus addresses.json list to call blast_radius on every address and assert no exception and no self-neighbour.
standouts: The load-bearing assertion is the controlled CO-REFERENCE + SELF-EXCLUSION check: A shares symbol S with B and ENG-feat, and A is not in its own neighbours.
The ROBUSTNESS section sweeps every real corpus address and treats any crash or self-inclusion as a failure.
STALE handling is explicitly mirrored against resolve_scope: absent/unreadable index yields stale=True, empty neighbours, and a regenerate note.
conventions: Imperative top-level test script rather than a unittest.TestCase or pytest function.
Inline section banners and verbose check messages that embed actual values.
Fixture builder returns a fresh Suite and monkey-patches a private _corpus_dir method.
Imports from store.fs_store, runtime.registry, and runtime.suite.
Uses broad Exception catch for the robustness loop, justified by the test goal.
concerns: Monkey-patching a private method _corpus_dir creates tight coupling to internal implementation details.
The robustness loop only prints the first two crash samples, so full failure detail may be hidden.
The test assumes design/_system/addresses.json exists and is readable; no graceful skip if it is absent.
The shared_only=True check only asserts B is still present, not that non-shared neighbours are actually filtered.
Catching Exception broadly could mask unexpected programming errors during the corpus sweep.
notes: Executable acceptance contract for the X9 blast_radius feature, written as a numbered checklist against a hand-built corpus fixture and a full real-corpus crash sweep.
questions: What is the complete return schema of blast_radius beyond the keys used here (symbols, neighbours, stale, note, address)?
How exactly should shared_only behave when a neighbour is reached through both shared and non-shared symbols?
Does resolve_scope always return a scope list of source files, or only for fixture-like symbols?
What is the intended boundary between X9 (co-reference inversion) and X10 (symbol-to-symbol graph) in the production implementation?
Is _corpus_dir a stable extension point, or is the monkey-patch only for testing?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring and the numbered checks directly implement the X9 acceptance criteria described in the header.
apparent_themes: Reuse existing substrate instead of building new indexes.
Acceptance tests as living implementation guides.
Preserve existing behaviour when adding sibling features.
Co-reference over semantic similarity for blast radius.
intention_signals: The file is trying to lock down an almost-free blast_radius capability that simply reads the existing referenced_by index backwards, ensuring it never silently fabricates answers, never crashes, and never breaks resolve_scope.
novelty: It frames acceptance testing as a numbered implementation guide (X9) with explicit forward references to X10 and X17, and includes a real-corpus non-crash oracle as a first-class acceptance criterion.
contribution: A runnable acceptance contract for blast_radius that pins self-exclusion, honest-empty results, S0 and stale failure modes, feature referrer inclusion, the shared_only parameter, and real-corpus crash-freedom; it also shows how to shadow _corpus_dir for controlled corpus fixtures.
summary_for_embedding: An imperative acceptance test for Suite.blast_radius that uses a hand-built code-symbols.json fixture and a real-corpus address sweep to verify co-reference neighbour inversion, self-exclusion, honest-empty handling, fail-loud malformed-address gating, stale-corpus legibility, feature referrer inclusion, shared_only support, and resolve_scope preservation.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an executable X9 acceptance checklist proving blast_radius safely inverts the existing referenced_by index into a self-excluded co-reference sibling set without new substrate or regressions.

symbols.does: assertion helper that increments PASS and prints ok
symbols.description: check(label, cond) is a minimal test assertion helper used throughout the file; it asserts the condition, increments a global PASS counter, and prints a formatted ok message with the label.

symbols.does: factory that creates a Suite with a temporary FsStore
symbols.description: fresh_suite() instantiates a new FsStore in a temporary directory and returns a Suite configured with the discovered node registry and nodes directory.

symbols.does: builds a temp code-symbols.json fixture and returns a Suite whose _corpus_dir points at it
symbols.description: with_fixture(symbols) writes the supplied symbols dictionary into a temporary design/_system/code-symbols.json file and returns a fresh Suite with its _corpus_dir method shadowed to read from that directory, enabling controlled tests for blast_radius and resolve_scope.
